**Title: The Horizon is the Floor**
**Author: Thomas**
**Date: October 14th**

They tell us the Earth is a round ball. A spinning marble in the void. It sounds like a trick of the mind, a story told to frighten the children of the surface. I have looked at the sky for forty years, and I have never seen a curve where there is none.

To them, the Earth is a sphere. To me, it is a plane. A flat, vast sheet of ice and rock, stretching out as far as the eye can see, with the sun and moon hanging above it like lamps in a hall. And I know the truth because I trust the things I see with my own eyes. I do not trust the math of the scholars. I trust the water.

They say a ball is round. But look at a cup of water. Look at the ocean. Water always finds its level. It does not want the bowl to be round; it wants the water to be flat. If the earth were a ball, the water would pour off the sides. Or, more accurately, the water would not be able to stay on the surface unless the earth was a sphere. But I see the water lying perfectly flat against the surface. If I stand at the edge of Lake Ontario and look at the horizon, I do not see a curve. I see a straight line. The horizon is the floor.

They tell me the planet spins. That we circle a sun and a moon in a great orbit. But consider the sensation of falling. When a ball spins, the ground beneath us should feel like it is shifting. But the ground is still. It is immovable. Gravity pulls me down, yet the earth remains a fixed, horizontal plane. If it were a spinning ball, the centrifugal force would tear the oceans away or push them to the other side. But they stay put.

They say we can see the curvature of the earth from a plane. I have watched the planes. I have watched the ships. They disappear over the horizon, not because of a curve, but because they are small. The larger they get, the less they seem to bend away. If the earth were round, they would shrink away from our view. But they do not. They stay level.

The sky is the ceiling. The water is the floor. The sun and moon are the fixtures. It is simple. It is stationary. It is the only place that makes sense.

When you look out, you do not see a sphere. You see a plane. And I know for certain that the world is flat.
